question_text: A coal power station burns fossil fuel to generate electricity. Which of the following best describes why coal is classified as a non-renewable energy resource?

options.label: A
options.text: Coal produces too much pollution when burned

options.label: B
options.text: Coal takes millions of years to form and we use it faster than it is replaced

options.label: C
options.text: Coal is only found in the United Kingdom

options.label: D
options.text: Coal cannot be recycled like other materials

explanation: Non-renewable resources like coal are finite because they take millions of years to form from dead organisms, but we burn them much faster than nature can replace them. Even though coal does pollute and has other drawbacks, the key definition of non-renewable is about how quickly it can be replenished.
